title: Undine
author: Friedrich de la Motte Fouqué
quotes: He shall not tell his story, father? He shall not? but it is my will. He shall! He shall in spite of you!
Undine! Undine! Pray come back!
The Lord was ever with me in those mysterious shades.
and had sunk beneath the watery mirror
her eyes, that reflected the blue of the lake and of the sky, smilingly upon us
the lake roared under the furious lashing of the wind; the trees of the little peninsula groaned from root to topmost bough, and bent, as if reeling, over the surging waters
Venture not, venture not, the old man, the stream, is full of tricks!
If thou art not really there, if thou art only floating about me like a mist, then may I too cease to live and become a shadow like thee, dear, dear Undine!
No world beyond these surrounding waters
Are you also angry, dear friend?
Spirits of the earth, if you wish to carry on your mischief, Kuhleborn shall teach you something better.
listen to me first, before you look angry, for your look of anger gives me pain, and you must not give pain to any creature who has done you no wrong
A stream ran out of the misty vale / Its fortunes to obtain, / the ocean's depths it found a home / And ne'er returned again.
I will give it you when you have quite alone explored the ill-famed forest, and can bring me tidings of its wonders.
into an ill-omened wood. The forest and its mysteries might have waited long enough for me!
The stems of the trees caught the red tints of the morning light which lay brightly on the green turf, the leaves seemed whispering merrily with each other
I was deep within the green shades
If I do not nibble away the branches up here, Sir Malapert, what shall we have to roast you with at midnight?
a brook of silvery brightness – thanks, dear brook.
False money! False coin! False coin! False money!
as to the lake, I know it well
remain with us
